# GEO — Generative Engine Optimization Skill

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)

Optimize content, site structure, and brand signals so **AI-powered answer engines** (Google AI Overviews, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Bing Copilot) cite, recommend, and surface your brand.

## What is GEO?

**Generative Engine Optimization** is the practice of optimizing your presence to appear in responses generated by AI-powered search systems. Instead of competing for SERP rankings alone, GEO ensures you become **part of the answer**.

> "Pages containing quotes and statistics had 30–40% higher visibility in AI responses." — [arXiv 2311.09735](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09735)

## What's Inside

```
geo-optimization/
├── SKILL.md                    # Core framework, quick-start audit, content patterns
├── references/
│   ├── playbook.md             # Tactical checklists for every content type
│   └── schema-patterns.md      # 9 ready-to-use JSON-LD templates
├── LICENSE
└── README.md
```

### SKILL.md — Core Framework
- **GEO = SEO × Extractability × Authority × Citation-worthiness**
- Quick-start audit (6 checks per page)
- Content patterns that get cited (answer-first, quote-heavy, table-friendly, concise)
- Authority & brand signals (on-site + off-site)
- Technical requirements (SSR, robots.txt for AI crawlers, performance)
- GEO vs SEO comparison
- Measurement approach (AI citations, share of voice, manual audit template)

### references/playbook.md — Tactical Playbook
- Page-level pre-publish checklist (content + technical)
- Content-type specific guides: blog, product, comparison, how-to, landing page
- Citation gap analysis methodology
- Priority matrix for closing gaps
- Monthly/quarterly/annual maintenance schedule

### references/schema-patterns.md — JSON-LD Templates
9 ready-to-use structured data templates:
1. `Article` / `WebPage` — foundation for any content page
2. `FAQPage` — directly feeds AI answer boxes
3. `HowTo` — step-by-step AI answers
4. `Product` — product recommendation AI answers
5. `ItemList` — comparison / roundup AI answers
6. `Organization` — sitewide entity
7. `Quotation` — attributed claims (emerging GEO signal)
8. `Dataset` — proprietary data (high differentiation)
9. `BreadcrumbList` — navigation + hierarchy signal

Includes Next.js integration example.

## Designed For

- **AI Agents** (OpenClaw, Codex, Claude Code, Cursor) — load as a skill for automated GEO work
- **SEO Teams** — use as a reference playbook for GEO strategy
- **Developers** — copy JSON-LD templates directly into your codebase
- **Content Marketers** — follow the content patterns and checklists

- [arXiv: GEO Research (2311.09735)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2311.09735)

## Quick Start

### Clone this repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/geo-optimization.git
cd geo-optimization
```

Read **`SKILL.md`** first — it is the agent entry point.

### Install as a Cursor skill (personal)

Copy or symlink the folder into your Cursor skills directory:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.cursor/skills
cp -R . ~/.cursor/skills/geo-optimization
# or: ln -s "$(pwd)" ~/.cursor/skills/geo-optimization
```

Restart Cursor or reload skills if your version requires it. The agent matches tasks using the YAML `description` in `SKILL.md`.

### Install as an OpenClaw skill

```bash
mkdir -p ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills
cp -R . ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/geo-optimization
```

Expected layout:

```
geo-optimization/
├── SKILL.md
└── references/
    ├── playbook.md
    └── schema-patterns.md
```

## Publish to GitHub

From your laptop (after replacing `YOUR_USERNAME` in the clone URL in this file if you edit it):

```bash
cd geo-optimization
chmod +x scripts/init-git.sh
./scripts/init-git.sh
```

Then create the remote repository and push:

```bash
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/geo-optimization.git
git push -u origin main
```

Using [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/):

```bash
./scripts/init-git.sh
gh repo create geo-optimization --public --source=. --remote=origin --push
```

To ship a zip without git history: archive the folder excluding `.git` (if any).
